dialogues: Here are all three patterns together. 2s are always even. 5s end in 0 or 5. 10s end in 0. These patterns aren't just facts. They're how you check your work.
Say you see this question: what is 8 times 5? And you have these choices.

Right. 13 ends in 3 and 42 ends in 2. Neither fits the 5s pattern, so they can't be right. That leaves 35 and 40. Both end in 0 or 5, so the pattern alone can't tell you which one. That's where skip-counting finishes the job.
